title: lastrea rusbyi
intro: Lastrea rusbyi, commonly known as Rusby's fern, is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the relief of inflammation and the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The therapeutic actions of Rusby's fern are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which can help to soothe and open airways. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Lastrea rusbyi can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. If ingested or applied topically in large amounts, it can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. In some cases, it may also lead to skin irritation, redness, and itching. Prolonged exposure or excessive use can result in more severe complications, including liver and kidney damage.
